blocks.content: Mr. Cole took Larry Jr. home. I waited for Jonathan outside the locker room. He came out still buzzing but lower than usual — the kind of lower that means he's thinking about something instead of just moving through it.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: The second catch I saw the corner sitting before the snap. Soon as the ball was out I knew.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: You heard me say it to —

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I know what you were doing.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: She knew about the corner sitting before you explained it.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Yeah I found that out AFTER I explained it. Which —

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You rechecked yourself when she was thirty feet away.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I was just — I was straightening my jersey.
That was maybe the most honest thing he'd said all night. I didn't make it a thing. Some things you leave alone.

blocks.prompt: Jonathan told Larry something he'd never say in front of anyone else. What does Larry do with it?

options.label: Keep it. This is what the friendship actually is.
File it for later. Not now — but eventually.
